changelog: Fix Android: a white bar showing a stretched 'SIERRO' wordmark left stuck at the top of the app after launch (visible above the Device list). Root cause: the native launch/splash theme (android/app/src/main/res/values/styles.xml, AppTheme.NoActionBarLaunch) mixed the modern androidx.core.splashscreen model (parent Theme.SplashScreen) with the LEGACY full-bleed android:background=@drawable/splash (a white full-screen SIERRO wordmark bitmap). On Android 12+ (API 31+) the OS ignores android:background for the splash and forces a 'background color + centered icon' model, so the white splash surface leaked as a top strip; and the launch theme was MISSING postSplashScreenTheme, the item androidx.core.splashscreen needs to hand the window back to the real dark no-ActionBar app theme — without it the splash never cleanly dismissed. The stretch/squish came from drawable/splash.png being a landscape 480x320 asset (every other density bucket was portrait) getting scaled to fill the thin strip. Reworked to the standard androidx config: windowSplashScreenBackground=@color/splashBackground (#FFFFFF, keeps the white brand), windowSplashScreenAnimatedIcon=@drawable/splash_logo (a newly generated, correctly-proportioned, centered SIERRO wordmark padded to sit inside the splash's circular icon mask without distortion), and postSplashScreenTheme=@style/AppTheme.NoActionBar so the splash reliably hands off to the dark app UI. Removed the legacy full-bleed android:background reference (the old per-density splash.png bitmaps are now unused). MainActivity's existing installSplashScreen()/setTheme(NoActionBar)/dark-window-background belt-and-suspenders is retained. Needs on-device confirmation on Android 12+/HyperOS.

Fix Android 12+ falsely demanding 'Turn on Location' before it can scan for Bluetooth devices. scanDevices() (bleProvision.ts) unconditionally pre-checked BleClient.isLocationEnabled() on every Android version and threw a 'Location services are off' error when it was off — which ProvisioningPage surfaces as the 'Bluetooth off' screen plus a 'Turn on Location (system setting)' toast. But the manifest already declares BLUETOOTH_SCAN with neverForLocation (and caps ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION at maxSdkVersion=30), so on Android 12+ (API 31+) BLE scanning is decoupled from location and works with Location OFF — the gate was a false block that made setup look broken. The Location pre-check is now version-gated: it only runs on Android 11 and below (where an off location genuinely yields zero scan results); Android 12+ scans regardless. Version detected from the WebView UA (androidMajorVersion()), so no new native dependency. Added a test for the 12+ case (does not consult isLocationEnabled, scans anyway) alongside the existing 11- behavior. Needs on-device confirmation on a real Android 12+ phone with Location off.

Server push (relay + poller): make Power Outage / Low Battery / Solar Status alerts reach the app even when it's fully CLOSED. The existing delivery chain (public/sw-push.js 'push' handler + the server/ relay's Web Push/FCM/APNs fan-out) only fired while the app process was alive; the official solar.siseli.com backend has no push API, so a killed app got nothing. Added the missing piece — an in-process poller in the self-hosted server/ relay (poller.js) that, per subscribed user, holds an access token, reads each device's /remote/device/state/latest, runs the same detection rules as the app (server/detect.js: outage keys / SOC<threshold / solar 0-crossing), and pushes new firings via the relay's channels with a 30-min per-device throttle. Multi-tenant auth uses a DEDICATED poller session: live testing of the backend showed refresh tokens are single-use (rotating one invalidates it and its access token) BUT one account supports multiple independent concurrent sessions — so at password login the app mints a second, independent session just for the poller (authApi.provisionPollerSession) and hands the relay only that session's refresh token (src/api/webPushApi.ts, one-time bootstrap consumed on first subscribe; the poller then owns and rotates it, never colliding with the app's own session). The relay stores it AES-256-GCM-encrypted at rest (server/crypto.js, TOKEN_ENC_KEY) and prunes it when the user unsubscribes everywhere. Email/SMS-code logins have no password to mint a second session, so those users keep foreground notifications only until they sign in with a password. This is a self-hosted backend addition — it changes ZERO official API endpoints and does not use the platform's device-automation (/instruction) service (whose actions control the device, not send pushes). Poller is off unless POLLER_ENABLED=true. See server/README.md.

Notifications: dedupe and filter the 'Active Now' alarm list. Duplicate alarms that resolve to the same description are now collapsed to one (e.g. lineLoss + mainsFailure both reading 'Mains power failure'), and a grid outage's correlated symptoms — Mains undervoltage (gridVoltLows) and Bypass undervoltage (bypassUndervoltageFault) — are hidden WHEN a 'Mains power failure' is also firing, so only the root cause shows. On their own (no mains failure) those undervoltage alarms still surface as genuine independent faults. Implemented as dedupeAndFilterAlarms() in alarmText.ts (with tests) and the 'N active now' count now reflects the filtered list.

Notifications: fix real-time (firing) alarms showing no actual description — the 'Active Now' rows rendered a generic 'Device Alarm' with an ACTIVE badge instead of what the alarm was. Root cause: the backend reports each firing alarm with key (code) / name (display), but NotificationsPage only read the legacy alarmCode / alarmMessage fields (empty against the real backend; only demo data fills them), so the title fell through to the generic fallback. Added a single shared resolver resolveAlarmText() in alarmText.ts that reads all field shapes with a sensible priority — curated English for a known code, then backend name, then alarmMessage, then a humanized code, then Alarm{id}/Device Alarm — and switched both NotificationsPage and the device-alarm push (deviceAlarmNotification.ts) to it so the alarm center and notifications show identical text. Added alarmText.test.ts (9 cases) covering the key/name, legacy, numeric and empty shapes.

Device Monitor Real-Time Power chart: add a left Y-axis scale so the curve's magnitude is readable — a 2-level gutter showing the max at the top and 0 at the baseline (Battery(SOC) tab reads 100% / 0; AC/Solar/Output read the window's peak in W / 0), with the horizontal grid lines realigned to those ticks. Labels are an HTML overlay (like the X-axis labels) so the preserveAspectRatio=none SVG doesn't distort them. Also hide the top-left data-status pill (the 'API · Npts' / 'cache · Npts' / 'p.N · Npts' point-count badge) as end-user noise; the centered loading spinner and the 'History unavailable' error badge are kept.

Remove the unused Overview dashboard page (OverviewPage, route /device/:id/dashboard). The route had no navigation path pointing at it anywhere in the app — it was dead/unreachable — so the page component and its route/import were deleted. The shared plumbing it referenced (bleDirect.ts BLE-direct, modbusProtocol.ts, useLiveDeviceStatus, decodePassthroughBase64, RealTimePowerChart, batteryTime, classifyBleError) is used elsewhere and is untouched; DeviceDetailPage (rendered as an overlay inside Overview) keeps its own /device/:id/settings route.

Device Monitor Real-Time Power chart: the Battery(SOC) tab now uses a FIXED 0–100% y-axis instead of auto-scaling to the window's max value, so a partial SOC range (e.g. 40–50%) no longer stretches to fill the chart height and misrepresent the level. Power tabs (AC/Solar/Output) still auto-scale to their own max.

Device Monitor (DeviceMonitorPage) Real-Time Power chart: the Battery tab now shows battery state-of-charge (SOC %, remainingBatteryCapacity) instead of charge/discharge power (W). The top-right badge reads the live SOC with a % unit, and the history curve plots the per-record SOC (already carried as HistoryPoint.soc). Implemented via an opt-in batteryAsSoc/batterySoc prop on the shared RealTimePowerChart so OverviewPage's Battery tab keeps showing battery power (W) unchanged; the AC/Solar/Output tabs are unaffected on both pages.

Device Info (DeviceDetailPage): fix the Temperature row showing a raw Celsius number labelled °F. The device reports battery temperature in °C (API cellTemperature1 / demo batteryTemp / powerStation.temperature are all Celsius), but the row previously printed the backend's Celsius valueDisplay as-is and its fallback appended a °F suffix without converting. It now converts to Fahrenheit via formatTemp(c,'F') — matching how OverviewPage and DevicePage already display Temperature — reading the numeric °C from realtime.batteryTemp → fields.batteryTemp.value → powerStation.temperature.

PWA app icon / favicon changed to the white-font SIERRO logo. Regenerated public/icon-192x192.png, icon-512x512.png and apple-touch-icon.png from the uploaded white wordmark on a solid black background (matches the manifest theme), and pointed index.html's favicon at the PNG icons (was an SVG). Native Android/iOS launcher icons and the in-app login wordmark are unchanged.

Android: further harden the 'large logo above the keyboard' fix. (1) Autofill: IMPORTANT_FOR_AUTOFILL_NO only opted out the WebView view itself, not its virtual children (the HTML inputs), so the Autofill Framework's branded suggestion strip could still appear on login-style forms; now use IMPORTANT_FOR_AUTOFILL_NO_EXCLUDE_DESCENDANTS on both the WebView and the window decor view to exclude the whole subtree. (2) Splash reveal: also set the window background to the dark app color (#141414) programmatically in MainActivity, so no @drawable/splash can be revealed behind the WebView when KeyboardResize.Body shrinks it, independent of theme-switch timing. Needs on-device confirmation.

Test/CI: add PG-01 Playwright e2e (permissions.e2e.spec.ts) asserting a fresh load shows no 'App Permissions' gate and lands on login, plus that the defunct sierro_permissions_asked flag no longer gates. Gated behind E2E_LOCAL=1, which makes playwright.config.ts start a `vite preview` server (bound to IPv4 127.0.0.1) so it runs against the local build rather than the deployed site.

Permissions are now requested purely on-demand — the first-launch 'App Permissions' onboarding screen (PermissionsGate) is removed entirely, so a fresh install / first open no longer shows a permission page and no longer batch-requests anything at startup. Each OS permission is now asked only when the user first uses that feature: Bluetooth when scanning/connecting to a device (BleClient.initialize() on scan), Camera when starting a QR scan (getUserMedia), and Notifications when a push toggle is turned on in Settings. Avatar change keeps using the system file picker (modern iOS/Android photo pickers grant per-selection and need no app-level permission prompt). Removed the App.tsx gate render + the sierro_permissions_asked localStorage flag; the wifi/storage best-effort web calls that the gate used to run at startup are simply no longer invoked. The per-feature request helpers in permissions.ts are unchanged.

Fix Bluetooth scanning/permission across Android, iOS and PWA. (1) Android 'stuck searching, no devices found': NativeBleProvisionManager.scanDevices() no longer OS-filters requestLEScan by namePrefix 'SSL_' — many Sierro units don't put an SSL_ name in the advertisement packet (it's only readable after a GATT connect), so the OS name filter matched nothing and the in-app list stayed empty forever. It now scans with no hardware name filter and filters client-side via isSierroScanResult(): a device qualifies if its name/localName starts with SSL_ OR it advertises the FEE7 provisioning service — mirroring connect()'s existing SSL_->service-UUID fallback. Same scanDevices() also powers Overview's Bluetooth Direct scan, so both surfaces are fixed. (2) Android scans now pre-check BleClient.isLocationEnabled() and surface an actionable 'turn on Location' hint, since Android silently returns zero scan results when system Location is off. (3) ProvisioningPage.handleScan now arms the 10s scan-stop timeout BEFORE starting the scan and, when nothing is found, shows a definite 'No nearby Sierro devices found' state instead of appearing to search forever. (4) PWA 'Open Settings' opened window.open('app-settings:') which just yielded a browser 'address error' tab — openAppSettings() now does nothing on web and returns false, and all five callers (ProvisioningPage/OverviewPage/DevicePage x2/PermissionsManager) show a toast telling the user to change the permission from the browser's own site-settings UI. (5) requestBluetooth() now offers the Android system 'turn on Bluetooth' dialog via BleClient.requestEnable() when the radio is off (skipped on iOS where requestEnable is unavailable); checkBluetooth()/requestBluetooth() share a bleInitialized flag so a passive check and a following scan don't re-enter the native permission callback. (6) classifyBleError() broadened to route 'not initialized'/'unavailable'/'location'/'not supported'/'unauthorized' messages to the correct permission-vs-bluetooth-off UI branch. Note: the client-side scan filter (SSL_ name OR FEE7 service) needs on-device confirmation; if a unit advertises neither signal, a future fallback can list all devices for manual pick.

Refactor: extract shared classifyBleError() helper from ProvisioningPage and OverviewPage into src/utils/permissions.ts. Both pages had identical string-matching heuristics for classifying BLE scan failures (permission denied vs. Bluetooth off vs. generic). Now exported as a single source of truth with BLErrorKind type and ClassifiedBLError interface, keeping the two BLE surfaces consistent and DRY.

Native BLE permission approach (correction of an earlier changelog claim that referenced BleClient.checkPermissions()/requestPermissions(), which do NOT exist on @capacitor-community/bluetooth-le@8.2.0 — unlike the Camera/Push plugins). On this plugin, BleClient.initialize({androidNeverForLocation:true}) IS the permission request: on Android it internally calls requestPermissionForAliases(BLUETOOTH_SCAN/BLUETOOTH_CONNECT) and rejects with 'Permission denied.' when not granted; on iOS it creates the CBCentralManager and triggers CoreBluetooth authorization. So checkBluetooth()/requestBluetooth() in permissions.ts gate on initialize() (+ isEnabled() for radio state), and ProvisioningPage detects a denied state by classifying the scan failure. PermissionsGate still defers the Bluetooth prompt until the user actually triggers a scan (avoids premature-prompt denial); the PWA path relies on navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() (not getAvailability()) as the real permission trigger.

Notifications page: remove alarm history — only show currently active (firing) alarms. Active alarm titles now resolve from the English alarmText dictionary (describeAlarmCode) first, falling back to the backend's alarmMessage only when the code isn't recognized. Removed the History section, Dismiss button, Load More pagination, and all associated store/API wiring (loadAlarms, dismissAlarm, AlarmItem type). The page is now purely real-time: it shows the live firingAlarms from the device state, or an empty state when nothing is firing.

Fix Android: large Sierro logo (splash drawable) taking over the screen when the soft keyboard appears. Root cause: the Activity theme (AppTheme.NoActionBarLaunch) permanently set android:background=@drawable/splash, and KeyboardResize.Body shrank the WebView to make room for the keyboard, exposing that splash drawable behind it. Fixed by switching the Activity theme to AppTheme.NoActionBar (dark #141414 background) via setTheme() after the splash dismisses in MainActivity.java, and adding android:windowSoftInputMode=adjustResize to the activity in AndroidManifest.xml so the framework properly coordinates window resizing with the keyboard.

Insights (StatsPage) Input vs. Output curve now sources real API history from POST /deviceState/attribute/record/list (the same doGetDeviceHistory endpoint the Device Monitor chart uses), and shows a flat 0 line when the device has no data for the selected period instead of falling back to synthetic demo numbers. Root cause found via live backend testing: the page called the OLD POST /deviceState/attribute/keys/history whose real response is actually a {page,count,total,list} record list (values at record top-level, often all null for idle/offline snapshots) — but aggregateHistory() read it as keyed arrays (raw['generationPower'] etc.), which never matched, so the chart ALWAYS fell through to fake DAY/WEEK/MONTH demo data. Verified against the real backend (device SSL_0F, 2026-07-14: 29 records → output 442W@18:00, correct SOC), and that record/list only returns rows that actually have values (empty list => genuine no-data => 0). Rewrote the data pipeline: fetchDeviceRecordHistory (ISO-8601 timestamps w/ timezone, paginated up to 300/page), fieldVal() reads the nested fields[key].value shape, and buildFrameFromRecords() buckets records onto a FIXED per-period axis (Day=24h / Week=Mon–Sun / Month=days / Range=days) zero-filling empty buckets. Removed the demo fallback (DAY_PAGES/WEEK_PAGES/MONTH_PAGES/getDemoChartFrame/smooth) and the DemoBanner from this page; added a lightweight Retry row shown only on an actual fetch error (not on genuine no-data). Scrub tooltip now labels values in W (they were power averages mislabeled as kWh).

Insights (StatsPage) Input vs. Output curve now sources real API history from POST /deviceState/attribute/record/list (the same doGetDeviceHistory endpoint the Device Monitor chart uses), and shows a flat 0 line when the device has no data for the selected period instead of falling back to synthetic demo numbers. Root cause found via live backend testing: the page called the OLD POST /deviceState/attribute/keys/history whose real response is actually a {page,count,total,list} record list (values at record top-level, often all null for idle/offline snapshots) — but aggregateHistory() read it as keyed arrays (raw['generationPower'] etc.), which never matched, so the chart ALWAYS fell through to fake DAY/WEEK/MONTH demo data. Verified against the real backend (device SSL_0F, 2026-07-14: 29 records → output 442W@18:00, correct SOC), and that record/list only returns rows that actually have values (empty list => genuine no-data => 0). Rewrote the data pipeline: fetchDeviceRecordHistory (ISO-8601 timestamps w/ timezone, paginated up to 300/page), fieldVal() reads the nested fields[key].value shape, and buildFrameFromRecords() buckets records onto a FIXED per-period axis (Day=24h / Week=Mon–Sun / Month=days / Range=days) zero-filling empty buckets. Removed the demo fallback (DAY_PAGES/WEEK_PAGES/MONTH_PAGES/getDemoChartFrame/smooth) and the DemoBanner from this page; added a lightweight Retry row shown only on an actual fetch error (not on genuine no-data). Scrub tooltip now labels values in W (they were power averages mislabeled as kWh).

Fix Device Monitor showing the previous device's data when switching between multiple devices: selectedDeviceState is a single shared store slot that selectDevice() never cleared, and loadDeviceState() only overwrites it on success — so after switching devices the SoC ring / AC / Solar / Output / chart kept showing the old device until the new fetch resolved (or forever if it failed). Now selectDevice() clears the previous device's cached state/details the moment you switch to a different device, and DeviceMonitorPage additionally ignores any store state whose deviceId doesn't match the current route id (belt-and-suspenders against a lagging response), so the displayed data always corresponds to the selected device. Also removed a redundant duplicate loadDeviceState call on the monitor page (selectDevice already loads it).

Fix v4.2.0 history chart showing all-zero data: verified live against the real backend that POST /deviceState/attribute/record/list nests each metric inside record.fields[key].value (e.g. fields.outputPower.value), NOT at the record's top level as v4.2.0 assumed — so every Battery/AC/Solar/Output point mapped to 0. useHistoryFetcher now reads from record.fields. Also confirmed the endpoint has NO batteryPower field at all, so the Battery tab now derives it as AC + Solar − Output (charge positive), matching the live-status path; verified against a real charging record (ac=399, solar=0, output=10 → battery=389). Corrected the DeviceAttributeRecord type in deviceApi.ts to reflect the real nested {time, deviceId, fields:{[key]:{value,...}}} shape.

Switch RealTimePowerChart history data source from POST /deviceState/attribute/keys/history to POST /deviceState/attribute/record/list (Dart doGetDeviceHistory pattern). The new endpoint returns full device attribute records per time point (paginated, ISO 8601 timestamps with timezone), fetching up to 80 records per page. Added fetchDeviceRecordHistory to deviceApi.ts with DeviceRecordHistoryRequest/Response types; updated useHistoryFetcher hook to build HistoryPoints from the record list by extracting the same 5 keys (generationPower/outputPower/remainingBatteryCapacity/batteryPower/exchangeChargingPower) from each DeviceAttributeRecord.

DeviceDetailPage: change all device display icons to pure white (were gray text-ink-6 / teal text-primary). Add custom image option — users can now pick any image from their photo library/camera to use as the device's display icon (saved as base64 per-device in localStorage, shown on both DeviceDetailPage icon picker and DevicePage device cards).

SettingPage: sync display name to auth account — reloadUserProfile now always overrides the IndexedDB-stored name with `authUser.account`, matching ProfileEditPage's read-only Username row (unified Account/Username/Name concept from v4.1.4). Previously the profile card could show a stale nickname from before the unification.

DevicePage: switch battery remainingBatteryCapacity (%) to Modbus passthrough (READ_ALL_STATUS, 0x0100+0x38 registers) instead of the cloud /state/latest API. Polls every 10s via passthrough (immediate first fetch on page enter, stops on leave); also updates AC/Solar/Output/Battery-Power/Temp from the same frame at no extra cost. The existing 60s cloud API poll still runs for device-params-modal fields and alarm state.

LoginPage: add Terms of Use & Privacy Policy checkbox (ticked by default, gates the Sign In button). Fix the bottom Terms/Privacy text overflowing the page on narrow screens — added break-words on the paragraph and whitespace-nowrap on the link text so the link phrases 'Terms of Use'/'Privacy Policy' stay intact while the surrounding text wraps naturally.

Rename app from 'Sierro Energy'/'Sierro App' to 'Sierro' everywhere: index.html title, Capacitor appName, Android strings.xml (app_name + title_activity_main), iOS CFBundleDisplayName, and PWA manifest name.

Three fixes. (1) Removed Onboarding's 'What should we call you?' name step — it only ever saved to local state with no server sync, so a name set there could silently diverge from ProfileEditPage's own account/nickname value; the display name is now simply the account entered at registration everywhere (ProfileEditPage's 'Name' row is now a read-only 'Username' sourced from `account`, matching RegisterPage/LoginPage's own labeling unified in v4.1.3). Onboarding now goes straight to 'Add your first device'. (2) Fix low-resolution/pixelated app-loading logo on Android: androidx.core:core-splashscreen was already a declared Gradle dependency and the app already ships a correctly-sized drawable/splash.png per density, but MainActivity.java never actually called SplashScreen.installSplashScreen(this) before super.onCreate() — on Android 12+ (API 31+, this app targets SDK 36) the theme's real system splash (Theme.SplashScreen) only takes over once that compat call is made; without it the OS falls back to its own default template, scaling up the small ~432px adaptive-icon foreground instead of using the proper splash asset, which is what read as a blurry logo. Regenerated all 30 icon/splash PNGs fresh via assets/gen-assets.mjs as a consistency check (visually confirmed crisp in isolation — not itself the root cause, the missing install call was). (3) Fix Android Bluetooth permission handling in Overview's Bluetooth Direct mode (v3.36.0): connecting via BLE just surfaced raw error text with no next step when the Nearby Devices permission was denied, even though Android won't re-prompt once denied — unlike ProvisioningPage's add-device flow, which already got this treatment in v3.34.2. Reused that same proven error classification (permission/denied vs. adapter-off/unavailable string matching) in OverviewPage's handleConnectDirect/handlePickDirectDevice/handleDisconnectDirect, now showing an actionable 'Open Settings' button on a permission denial instead of a dead-end error banner.

Six auth/UX fixes. (1) Register: 'Verification code error' could show even with a correctly-typed code — confirmed live against the backend that this fires whenever the submitted code doesn't match the *current* captchaId session, including on a retry after a wrong first attempt (the session can already be consumed). Added a synchronous double-tap guard on 'Obtain Code' (the sending-state alone has a render-lag window a fast tap could slip through, silently requesting a second code and orphaning the first email's code), and now force a fresh code (clear captchaId/code/countdown) after any code-mismatch error instead of letting the user retry against a dead session. (2) Login with email+password could land on demo/guest data: authStore.login()'s success path cleared isGuest but never called exitDemoMode() — isDemoMode and the demo device list persist in localStorage, so a user who'd previously used 'Continue as Guest' would still see that stale demo state after a real login. This was flagged as RELEASE_PLAN P1-2 but never actually fixed; fixed now in both the password-login path (authStore.ts) and the email+code login path (LoginPage.tsx), which had the identical bug via its own separate setState call. (3) Unified Username labeling: RegisterPage labeled the login identifier 'Account' while its own placeholder/errors and LoginPage called it 'Username' — relabeled for consistency. Also found and fixed a related gap: OnboardingPage's name step only saved to local IndexedDB, never syncing to the server nickname ProfileEditPage reads/writes, so a name set during onboarding could silently disappear/diverge later — now also calls updateUserInfo. (4) Verification code emails arrived in Chinese or English inconsistently — apiClient.ts never sent an Accept-Language header, so the backend likely auto-detected language from the requesting device/browser's own locale; now explicitly fixed to en-US on every request, matching the app's English-only UI. (5) Android: the OS Autofill Framework was showing a small suggestion strip branded with the app's launcher icon above the keyboard on login-style forms (WebViews opt into this by default) — MainActivity.java now explicitly opts the WebView out via View.IMPORTANT_FOR_AUTOFILL_NO (API 26+). (6) 'Obtain Code' button text could visually overflow past its rounded input container on narrow screens — classic flexbox bug (a flex-1 input without min-w-0 won't shrink below its placeholder's intrinsic width); fixed on LoginPage, RegisterPage, and ForgotPasswordPage's equivalent code-input rows.

Terms of Use and Privacy Policy no longer render local in-app text — every link (Login, Register, Settings, Data Export) now opens the marketing site directly (src/config/legalLinks.ts: TERMS_URL -> https://www.sierro.us/pages/terms, PRIVACY_URL -> https://www.sierro.us/pages/policy, target=_blank/window.open). Deleted TermsPage.tsx/PrivacyPage.tsx and the /terms, /privacy routes since nothing renders them anymore. Updated the two e2e tests that used to click through and check in-app navigation to instead verify the link href/target, since a target=_blank click no longer navigates the current page. Also fixed docs/PLAY_SUBMISSION.md's Privacy Policy URL, which still pointed at the now-dead GitHub Pages /#/privacy route — would have been a real Play Console submission problem if left stale.

Fix Forgot Password always failing with a misleading 'Invalid verification code or the code has expired' error regardless of whether the code was correct: verified live against the backend that /user/reset/password requires a non-blank `account` field, but ForgotPasswordPage.tsx sent account='' (omitted from the request by authApi.ts's resetPassword()), which unconditionally failed backend validation with 'illegal argument: account must not be blank' before the verification code was ever checked — the client's generic illegalArgument-regex error mapping then displayed that as an invalid/expired-code message. Fixed by sending the user's typed email as `account` (confirmed live that the backend accepts email as an account identifier here, same as the login page's 'Username or email' field, and that doing so gets past this validation stage).

Fix DevicePage device-card model label: the cloud's `model` field returns null for real devices (confirmed via live API testing), so cards fell through to a generic 'Sierro' label instead of 'Sierro 1000'/'Sierro 2000'. Now falls back to inferring the model from the device's own reported `ratedPower` (500W board = Sierro 1000, 1000W board = Sierro 2000, per src/data/deviceModels.ts's spec table) before the generic fallback — a device-reported hardware value instead of the unreliable cloud model string.

Add a Device Alarms push notification toggle (Settings > Push Notifications): the cloud alarm center (Notifications page, /alarm/query/list + firingAlarms) always showed every alarm type the backend reports, but the *push*-notification side only ever watched three specific hand-built local conditions (Power Outage, Low Battery, Solar Status) — any other firing alarm (over-temp, cell fault, bus overvoltage, etc.) never produced a notification even though it was visible in-app. Added checkAndNotifyDeviceAlarms (src/utils/deviceAlarmNotification.ts), which notifies for any firing alarm not already covered by the power-outage-specific matcher, using the same dedup-by-alarmId pattern as the existing power-outage notifier and alarmText.ts's ~40-entry code-to-text dictionary (falling back to a humanized version of unknown codes, never a blank notification). Off by default like the other three toggles. Also fixed a real gap found while wiring this in: the Power Outage push notification fired unconditionally on every device-state poll regardless of its own Settings toggle (Low Battery and Solar Status were correctly gated, Power Outage wasn't) — now all four categories are consistently gated by their own toggle before firing.

Documentation overhaul (v4.0.0): consolidated and de-duplicated the project's docs so they reflect current reality instead of a mix of old and new. Deleted three docs whose entire content had silently become stale — docs/API_STATUS.md (an API-integration todo list where nearly everything it flagged as 'not yet implemented' — history data, add device, reset/change password, email/SMS login — had actually been implemented for a long time), docs/QA_TEST_REPORT.md (a point-in-time static audit snapshot from v3.13.0 describing defects — a hardcoded backdoor test account, an unreachable DataExportPage, a dead /peak-shaving route — that were all independently verified fixed), and docs/TEST_CHECKLIST.md (an older, less-complete duplicate of docs/TEST_PLAN.md, which stays as the one canonical test matrix). Rewrote docs/PRODUCT_SPEC.md's route table and several page descriptions, which had drifted from reality: it described OverviewPage as living at '/' with an 'InsightsPage.tsx' that never existed under that name (it's StatsPage.tsx at /insights; OverviewPage is at /device/:id/dashboard, DevicePage's /devices is the actual post-login default), claimed BleDebugPage does 'Modbus command passthrough' (it only does BLE provisioning — PassthroughPage/DebugParamsPage do the real Modbus work), and claimed PermissionsManager had been removed (it hasn't — it's the live Settings permission-test UI). Added missing coverage throughout: Bluetooth Direct Mode (new in v3.36.0), the connectionStore/provisionStore Zustand stores, the NATIVE_PUSH_READY vs PUSH_ENABLED distinction, and the Android R8/native-debug-symbols fix — cross-referenced consistently across CLAUDE.md, PRODUCT_SPEC.md, RELEASE_PLAN.md, TEST_PLAN.md, NATIVE_SETUP.md, and API_REFERENCE.md so the same fact isn't stated three different ways in three different files. RELEASE_PLAN.md's unit-test line (previously 'currently 0') now reflects the real count (7 files / 68 tests); its milestone table now shows Android's release-AAB pipeline as done, leaving iOS TestFlight signing as the sole remaining ship-blocker. Added a 'Documentation map' section to CLAUDE.md so future changes land in the right doc instead of spawning a fourth copy of the same fact.

Add Bluetooth Direct mode to Overview (manual toggle, top-right Bluetooth button): when the cloud is unreachable — or for pure local/offline use — you can now connect straight to the device over BLE and get live Battery/AC/Solar/Output/Temperature readings plus basic control (Sleep Mode, AC output, DC/USB output) without going through the cloud API at all. Built on the existing BLE provisioning channel (src/protocols/bleProvision.ts, FEE7/FED5/FED6 GATT service) by adding uartPassthrough() (CID 30024/30025, a raw UART/Modbus passthrough command already scaffolded in types/protocol.ts and confirmed against the vendor's own reference app) and a new src/protocols/bleDirect.ts that feeds the existing Modbus frame builders (modbusProtocol.ts FRAMES/decodeLiveStatus) through it. useLiveDeviceStatus.ts gained a third 'ble' source alongside fastReport/passthrough, reusing all its existing safeguards (visibility-gated polling, no overlapping requests, fail-streak clears stale values). Two intentional scope differences from cloud control, both labeled in the UI: (1) Ports show AC Output + DC/USB Output (2 switches, matching the real register 0x0080) instead of the cloud's AC1/AC2/USB (3 switches, which only the cloud/firmware can map); port state is optimistic (reflects your last action, not a live read — the register map has no separate port-status read). (2) Sleep Mode applies the sleep/wake charge-power register (0x0085, via the same per-model wattage already used by the time-window scheduler in useSleepModeScheduler.ts) immediately once, rather than running the full scheduled routine. Needs verification on real hardware before wide release: the UART parity-bit string is set to 'None' per Modbus RTU convention (9600-8-N-1, already documented in modbusProtocol.ts) but wasn't independently confirmed against firmware — a wrong value fails safely as a CRC-rejected response, not a device-affecting write.

Fix Android crash when granting the Notifications permission: android/app/google-services.json was never committed (build.gradle already skips the google-services plugin when it's absent), so FirebaseApp is never initialized natively, yet requestNotifications() (src/utils/permissions.ts, hit from the onboarding Permissions gate and Settings > Permissions) and initNativePush() (src/utils/nativePush.ts, hit after login once v3.35.5 turned VITE_PUSH_ENABLED on for all production builds) both unconditionally called PushNotifications.register() right after permission grant — which calls FirebaseMessaging.getInstance(), throwing because no default FirebaseApp exists. Added a new NATIVE_PUSH_READY flag (src/config/webPush.ts, default false — flip via VITE_NATIVE_PUSH_READY=true once google-services.json/APNs credentials are actually in place) and gated both register() call sites on it. The OS permission prompt itself still appears and resolves normally either way; only the unsafe native FCM token registration is now skipped until real credentials exist.

Android release build: enable R8/minifyEnabled + shrinkResources (was off, so no mapping.txt was ever produced) and set ndk.debugSymbolLevel='SYMBOL_TABLE' on the release build type, fixing the two Play Console upload warnings ('no deobfuscation file' / 'native debug symbols not uploaded') and shrinking the AAB. AGP embeds the mapping file directly in the bundle for AAB uploads, so no separate manual upload step is needed. Added a Capacitor-safe proguard keep block (plugin classes, @CapacitorPlugin/@PluginMethod, WebView JS bridge) as a safety net alongside the consumer-rules.pro most Capacitor/plugin AARs already ship.

Fix Overview page leaking raw Chinese backend field names: the Energy Flow Detail Groups section (Grid/PV Panel/Battery/Load Status) rendered the /deviceState/simple/energy/flow/v1 API's group.name and item.nameDisplay/valueDisplay directly, which the backend returns pre-localized to Chinese (e.g. "电网状态", "交流输入电压", "有交流充电中") — this app is English-only and DebugParamsPage is the only page meant to show raw register text, per the label canon in CLAUDE.md. Added translateEnergyFlowGroupLabel/FieldLabel/FieldValue/Unit in src/utils/localization.ts mapping every group/field key to an English label, deriving boolean/enum values (AC Charging, On/Off, Active/Idle, etc.) from the raw "0"/"1" value instead of trusting the backend's Chinese phrase, and translating the one non-Latin unit seen (分钟 -> min). Verified against a live capture of the endpoint: every group, field, and value now renders in English.

Re-enable Push Notifications (Power Outage / Low Battery / Solar Status) in Settings: VITE_PUSH_ENABLED=true is now set in every production build (GitHub Pages, Android APK/AAB, iOS). This had been off by default since P0-4 (v3.34.0) pending native push readiness; toggling these switches still works safely without a configured VAPID key or backend endpoints — enableWebPush()/syncWebPushSubscription() no-op gracefully when unconfigured, and the in-app low-battery/outage monitor already delivers local notifications independent of server push. Also added the iOS Push Notifications capability (App.entitlements with aps-environment, wired into both build configs) so native APNs registration has the entitlement it needs once backend credentials land.

Device Monitor's Real-Time Power chart now uses the exact same chart as Overview (extracted into a shared RealTimePowerChart component), instead of two divergent implementations that had drifted apart: Device Monitor previously buffered its own local-storage sample cache with a fixed 0-1000W/0-100% y-axis and no zoom, while Overview read real API timestamps with a fixed 12am-4am-8am-12pm-4pm-8pm-12am x-axis and pinch/wheel zoom. Both pages now share the same data source, gestures, colors, and status badges. Note: Device Monitor's Battery tab now plots battery charge/discharge power (W) to match Overview, not state-of-charge (%) as before.

Fix descenders (j/g/p/q/y) getting visually clipped at the bottom of text inputs (Login, Register, Forgot Password, Onboarding name field, BLE Wi-Fi provisioning, BLE debug): the shared text-body-lg token's locked line-height (1.2) renders an <input>'s line box at exactly 19.2px for 16px text with no headroom, and unlike a <div>, an input clips rather than growing to fit its content. Added a scoped override (line-height 1.5) for input/textarea elements only, so the shared text-body-lg token itself is untouched everywhere else it's used.

Data-fetching/UX audit — two silent-failure bugs fixed: (1) Device list load failures (network error or non-success API code) were swallowed inside deviceStore.loadDevices with no error surfaced, so DevicePage's error banner and empty-state Retry button never appeared — a failed refresh just left the user staring at a stale or empty list with no explanation. loadDevices now records the failure in a new deviceError store field and DevicePage propagates it. (2) When the daily/period history fetch failed with nothing cached, StatsPage (Insights) and OverviewPage (dashboard chart) silently fell back to synthetic getDemoChartFrame()/empty-chart data with zero indication it wasn't real — a logged-in user could see fabricated power numbers with no way to tell. Insights now shows the existing DemoBanner ("DEMO MODE — Showing simulated data", with Retry) in that case, and the Overview chart shows a "History unavailable" badge instead of the ambiguous no-data placeholder. All three verified live (Playwright + mocked API failures) before/after; 62/62 unit tests and full route sweep still pass.

Fix Overview/Device-Monitor deep link bouncing to Device list: the back-gesture interceptor on OverviewPage and DeviceMonitorPage treated every popstate event as a real back-swipe and immediately redirected to /devices — but popstate can also fire from a non-back-gesture navigation into these pages (e.g. a deep link from a push notification, or any hard hash navigation rather than in-app router.navigate()), so landing straight on /device/:id/dashboard could bounce right back to the device list. The interceptor now only redirects if the address is still the guarded page's own URL when popstate fires (i.e. the popped entry really is our own placeholder), otherwise it lets the router render the new route normally.

Fix Sleep Mode not writing AC charge power (0x0085): the scheduler only sent at time-window edges and its enforce effect had empty deps, so toggling Sleep Mode on/off or editing the sleep window sent nothing until a boundary crossing happened to occur while the Device Info page was open — you'd see no 0x85 write. Now the power is applied immediately on enable and whenever sleepFrom/sleepTo change (sleep phase -> Sierro 1000 150W / Sierro 2000 300W; wake phase -> 400W / 800W), turning Sleep Mode off restores the wake power, and the boundary enforcement still runs. Locked the per-model values with a unit test (getPowers)

Fix Android 'cannot get Bluetooth permission': on native the provisioning screen initialized bleStatus to 'ready' and never routed to the actionable Permission Required / Bluetooth Off screens, so when the BLE (Nearby devices) permission was denied the user only saw a transient error toast with no way to fix it. Now a scan failure is classified: permission/denied -> Permission Required screen with Open Settings + a 'try again' button and Android 12+ 'Nearby devices' wording; adapter off/unavailable -> Bluetooth Off screen. Note: BLE runtime permission/scan is now exercisable on-device for the first time; if the OS prompt still doesn't appear, grant Nearby devices via Settings using the new button

Fix Android/iOS login timeout: enable CapacitorHttp so native fetch/XHR go through the native HTTP stack instead of the WebView. In the WebView the app's origin is https://localhost, which the API's CORS allowlist rejects, and the Origin/Referer headers apiClient sets are dropped as forbidden headers — so login (and every API call) hung until the 10s timeout. Native HTTP bypasses CORS and actually sends those headers, matching what the backend expects. Web is unaffected (CapacitorHttp is native-only). Requires a rebuilt app; the next Android APK from CI includes the fix

Push cropped for first release (P0-4): new PUSH_ENABLED flag (src/config/webPush.ts, default false) because remote push cannot work yet (no APNs entitlement, no google-services.json, backend token endpoints unimplemented). While off: the Settings 'Push Notifications' section is hidden (tree-shaken from the production bundle), no push init on login (App.tsx), and the onboarding permission gate no longer requests notification permission (PermissionsGate). This removes the store-rejection risk of asking for notification permission for a feature that can't deliver. Flip VITE_PUSH_ENABLED=true once the backend + APNs/FCM credentials are ready to restore the full path (code retained). Updated docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md P0-4 status

Brand assets (P0-3): replace the Capacitor placeholder app icon and splash on BOTH platforms with the SIERRO wordmark (white background, black text, per brand). Generated the full set with sharp from assets/ sources (assets/gen-assets.mjs, reproducible): iOS AppIcon 1024 + Splash imageset 2732; Android launcher icons + round + adaptive foreground (wordmark scaled into the adaptive safe zone so circular/rounded masks don't clip it) across mdpi..xxxhdpi, and portrait/landscape splashes at every density. Adaptive icon background is white. Splash screen backgroundColor set to #FFFFFF to match. Note: a wide wordmark as a square icon reads small on phone home screens — an 'S' monogram would be larger; kept the full wordmark per request

Post-review fixes (7-dimension release audit). Two R1 realtime regressions fixed: (1) in fast-report mode an offline device kept showing 'Connected' with frozen readings because /state/latest returns the last cloud state forever — online status is now derived only from the cloud isOnline flag plus successful PASSTHROUGH round-trips; (2) a fast-report session could be orphaned (device stuck uploading at high rate) if the page was left mid-start — the active flag is now set before the start await so cleanup always stops it. Privacy honesty: removed false usage-analytics claims from the privacy policy and the no-op Anonymous Analytics toggle from Data Export; export stamps the real app version. Native readiness: Android versionName injected from src/version.json + versionCode from CI, allowBackup=false + data_extraction_rules; iOS PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy added. Release engineering: E2E CI now builds/serves and tests THIS commit (was testing production); real-account E2E creds moved to env and skipped when absent. Updated docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md with all 7 dimensions

Release hardening (RELEASE_PLAN M2). P0-2: 'Delete Account' now actually calls the deleteAccount API (POST /user/logout/account) with loading state and error handling, then clears the session — previously the reachable flow only logged out, which fails Apple 5.1.1(v) and Play's account-deletion policy. P0-5: added NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription/NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription to iOS Info.plist (the linked @capacitor/camera plugin references photo-library APIs -> avoids ITMS-90683 upload rejection). P1-5: Modbus Debug / Debug Params / BLE Debug pages and their Device-Info entry links are now hidden in release builds behind DEV_TOOLS_ENABLED (import.meta.env.DEV || VITE_ENABLE_DEV_TOOLS==='true'), so consumers can't reach raw-register/passthrough control surfaces. P1-6: set ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false in Info.plist (standard HTTPS/MD5 only) to stop the export-compliance prompt on every upload

Security: remove public/api-test.html — a debug console with a hardcoded AppSecret that was bundled into dist and published to GitHub Pages (publicly reachable). Also removed the local test_api.py containing a real account password. The AppSecret should be treated as leaked and rotated with the platform vendor. Added docs/RELEASE_PLAN.md: full App Store / Play Store readiness review and phased development plan (P0 blockers through P3 release engineering)

UI polish + iOS/Android adaptation (all 4 batches). Batch 1: transparent tap-highlight (no more gray flash on Android taps), overscroll-behavior locks page rubber-banding, touch-action manipulation on controls, user-select off on buttons/nav, Android windowBackground/statusBar/navigationBar unified to #141414 (kills launch white-flash), viewport allows pinch-zoom again (accessibility), tabular-nums extended to battery %% and Smart Schedule savings. Batch 2: new native UX bootstrap - StatusBar styled (light icons on dark), Android hardware back button handled (inner pages go back, root pages minimize the app instead of exiting), Keyboard resize=body + focused input scrolled into view; haptic feedback on toggles (light) and pull-to-refresh (medium) via @capacitor/haptics with web vibrate fallback. Batch 3: removed all 15 remaining transition-all (explicit properties only), Overview sections now stagger-enter at 100ms steps. Batch 4: iOS project generated (npx cap add ios) with Bluetooth/Camera/Local-Network usage descriptions in Info.plist, plus a macOS CI workflow building an unsigned simulator binary to keep the iOS project compiling. Android now registers 9 native plugins. Verified: typecheck clean, build ok, 25/25 local e2e passed

R1 realtime pipeline: new useLiveDeviceStatus hook is the single live-status entry for Overview. It probes platform fast-report support on entering the page; if supported it starts a fast-report session (device raises its cloud reporting rate) and polls the cheap /state/latest endpoint every 5s instead of occupying the Modbus passthrough channel; unsupported/old-firmware devices automatically fall back to the previous passthrough READ_ALL_STATUS polling. Fast report is stopped on leaving the page. Same safeguards as before: visibility-gated, no overlapping requests, live values cleared after 3 consecutive failures. Protocol decode verified with synthetic frames (good/corrupted-CRC/truncated + Int16 temp + 0x0133 state enum), typecheck clean, 25/25 local e2e passed

Engineering hardening (P1-P3 from architecture review). Typecheck: fixed invalid tsconfig ignoreDeprecations that made tsc unrunnable, fixed all 42 type errors (demoData drifted from DeviceListItem: ids/station/dtu ids are strings, state is a numeric enum, applyMode is a string; LucideIcon prop types; AppSettings.privacyAcknowledged; LoginData.email; PeakValleyEnableRequest.deviceId; PWA virtual module types; NotificationOptions.renotify), and build now runs tsc --noEmit first so type errors fail CI

API client: 5xx and timeouts now retry with backoff (previously only DNS/connection errors retried); HTTP 401 responses now go through the token-refresh flow too (previously only business-code 401/1001/1002 did)

Protocol layer: new decodePassthroughBase64() is the single entry for passthrough base64->registers decode (CRC-checked); OverviewPage live poll and deviceStore rated-params fetch both use it instead of duplicated inline decoding

Polling: Overview's 4 pollers (5s passthrough / 30s state / 60s alarms / 60s energy flow) now skip ticks while the page is hidden, and the 5s passthrough poll no longer issues overlapping requests when a call takes >5s
Fix native BLE scan leak: closing the provisioning sheet now stops the LE scan and clears the 10s auto-stop timer (destroyProvisionManager also stops scanning)
Fix QR Rescan frozen frame: the camera stream is re-acquired after a successful scan stopped it, so Rescan actually scans again
Realtime simulator now runs only in Demo (guest) mode - a real logged-in session no longer writes synthetic power history to IndexedDB
Cleanup: removed dead hooks (useDeviceWebSocket, useOfflineSync) and root-level log/capture artifacts (push_log*.txt, captured-*.json, test-login-real.mjs)
Signing credentials can now be injected at build time via VITE_IOT_APP_ID / VITE_IOT_APP_SECRET (localStorage override still wins; bundled fallback documented as public)

Data-integrity fixes (P0 from architecture review): 1) passthrough live decode now rejects frames with bad CRC (OverviewPage 5s poll + rated-params fetch) so corrupted responses can no longer show garbage SOC/power or mark the device online; 2) Overview live values (livePt) are cleared after 3 consecutive passthrough failures so a disconnected device falls back to cloud state instead of freezing on the last frame as 'Connected'; 3) fix System State Machine (0x0133) decode — it is a numeric enum, not a bitmask, so states like Charging(6) no longer display as Power-Up

Fix Wi-Fi Setup doing nothing when tapped: the Add-Device sheet's 'Wi-Fi Setup' option had no action (just closed the sheet). It now launches the BLE provisioning flow — scan nearby devices over Bluetooth (added or not), pick one, and set its Wi-Fi SSID/password. Also fixed handleBleScan dead-ending on the native app (navigator.bluetooth is absent in the WebView) by going straight to provisioning on native, and the device-scan screen now auto-starts the Bluetooth scan on entry
Email-code login: Obtain Code now requests the LOGIN captcha intent (CaptchaIntent.LOGIN '3'), not the register captcha, so the emailed code is the correct login verification code

Fix Android APK not requesting Bluetooth permission: the git-tracked Capacitor build files (capacitor.settings.gradle / capacitor.build.gradle) were stale and listed only 2 of 6 plugins, and capacitor.plugins.json was git-ignored — so a build that skipped `cap sync` shipped an APK with NO native BLE/camera/push/local-notification plugin. BleClient.initialize() then failed and the runtime permission prompt never appeared. Regenerated the build files (all 6 plugins) and now track capacitor.plugins.json so the native plugins are always compiled in and registered
Sleep Mode screen: translate remaining Chinese to English (AC 充电功率 -> AC charging power; 降至/恢复 -> arrows)

Overview: while on the page, poll live params via Modbus passthrough (READ_ALL_STATUS 0x0100) every 5s and update AC/Solar/Output/Battery/Temp (decodeLiveStatus); stops when leaving the page. Live values take priority over the 30s cloud state; a passthrough reply marks the device online
Battery Priority now drives PV/Battery Priority (0x86) via passthrough: Savings -> enable 0x01AA, Backup -> disable 0xAA01 (sent on Save alongside setWorkMode)

Sleep Mode now drives AC realtime charge power (0x0085) via passthrough: at sleep time -> Sierro 1000 150W / Sierro 2000 300W; at wake time -> 400W / 800W. Uses the device model profile (was writing rated 0x0024 and 1000W for 2000)

Passthrough protocol: add registers 0x54 (PV/Battery Priority Min SOC, %, default 30), 0x85 (AC realtime charge power, W, default 400), 0x86 (PV/Battery Priority on=0x01AA/off=0xAA01); new FRAMES + Passthrough page presets + REG_DESC parsing

Overview: add pull-to-refresh (fetches live AC input / Solar input / AC output + online state); 30s poll now also refreshes device details so isOnline stays fresh (params no longer appear stuck). Confirmed field mapping is correct: AC=exchangeChargingPower, Solar=generationPower, Output=outputPower
Fix: model picker sheet was rendered in the Device Settings screen, so tapping Model on Device Info did nothing until going back; moved the sheet into the Device Info screen where the Model row lives

Device Info: tap Model to manually select Sierro 1000/2000 — applies that model's default rated params (capacity/power/charge power/battery type/health) and serial prefix to the current device, for devices added before model selection existed

Add Device: choose model (Sierro 1000 / 2000) -> auto-generate serial number + default rated params (Rated Power, Rated Capacity, Rated Charging Power, Battery Type LFP, Battery Health 100%); saved per device and shown on Device Info (new Rated Charging Power row). BLE param sync now merges, preserving the model profile

Notifications: parse alarm code/key into readable text (alarmText dictionary + humanize fallback) so all history records show a description, not just Mains power failure; harden history filter so empty-key records are never hidden; same parsing in Overview alerts

WiFi setup: native app now scans and lists nearby BLE (SSL_) devices in-app (BleClient.requestLEScan); tap a device to connect and proceed to its Wi-Fi SSID/password config. Web keeps the OS Bluetooth picker. Fixed native being blocked by the navigator.bluetooth permission check

Device list power toggle now actually controls the device via Modbus passthrough: ON writes 0x0080<-0x01AA (AC_POWER_ON), OFF writes 0x0080<-0xAA01 (AC_POWER_OFF); optimistic UI with rollback + error on failure, disabled while in flight
Revert pure-black backgrounds back to #141414 (base token/body/manifest/native + page roots ink-13->ink-12); cards remain ink-10. Kept the e2e selector fix
Bottom nav: reduce bottom gap 8px -> 4px (sit closer to the bottom edge)
All page backgrounds now pure black (#000000): base token/body/manifest/native + page roots ink-12->ink-13; cards stay ink-10 for contrast
Fix e2e: device-card check matched the raw hex class [class*=262626] which the token sweep removed; now matches the ink-10 token class
Bottom nav: align to bottom edge on iOS (drop safe-area-inset reservation; fixed 8px gap) so it no longer floats above the Home indicator

Radius unification: migrate all legacy rounded-md/lg/xl to canonical scale (md->m 8px, lg/xl->l 12px) so every surface uses s/m/l/pill; cards & dialogs now uniform 12px, inputs/tiles consistent (modal sheet tops keep their 28px arbitrary radius)
Color tokens: snap off-palette grays to ink scale (#A0A0A5/#AEAEB2->ink-6, #636366->ink-7 per config intent) and unify #FF3530->danger; removes off-palette colors
Design-token sweep (components): same value-identical hex->token + rounded-sm->rounded-m pass over src/components

Design-token sweep (pages): replace value-identical hardcoded hex (#262626->ink-10, #BFBFBF->ink-6, #01D6BE->primary, etc.) with tokens; rounded-sm->rounded-m (both 8px). Zero visual change, consistency only
UI polish: enlarge Overview sheet close buttons 28->36px (tap target) + scale-on-press; replace transition-all on shared Button and BottomNavigation with specific properties

Fix asymmetric card corners: rounded-l(12px)/rounded-s(4px) tokens collided with Tailwind built-in side utilities (rounded-l=left, rounded-s=start), making left corners 4px and right 12px; force all-corners in index.css
Bottom nav: position adapts to device safe-area (calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom)+12px)); fixes inconsistent floating gap on devices without a safe-area inset (was conflicting pb-6 + 34px fallback)

WiFi setup: auto-scan AP list on entering Select Wi-Fi (no more manual Scan Again); retry once when first scan returns empty (device scan latency); stay on list to pick SSID (was wrongly jumping to password); dedupe/empty-SSID filter
Fix BLE add-device "serial number cannot be empty": provisioning now sends deviceSerialNumber=DTUID + isVirtualSerialNumber (no separate inverter SN available during BLE setup)
Fix: BLE-added device not appearing in list — add-device sent wrong payload (duid/name); now sends deviceName+dtuDtuid+stationId (reuses a station or creates one) and surfaces real bind failures
Remove dead USB/Web-Serial code: delete serialModbus.ts, useProtocol.ts, useConnectionStatus.tsx; strip serial from connectionStore + ProtocolType
Add Device: remove Connect via USB (RS485) option; WiFi provisioning auto-retries a command once after reconnecting if GATT drops mid-write

Fix WiFi setup GATT disconnect: reconnect-on-demand before each BLE provisioning command (Web + native) so idle GATT drops auto-recover; native scan falls back to service-UUID filter when SSL_ prefix misses; friendlier disconnect error copy

WiFi setup (BLE provisioning) now works on native iOS/Android: bleProvision.ts refactored to dual backend (Web Bluetooth + @capacitor-community/bluetooth-le) behind a shared base class; same FEE7/FED5/FED6 protocol, AES+Base64+packet framing, CID command set per spec v2.9
Android: CI builds a downloadable debug APK and publishes it as a GitHub Release asset (apk-v<version>)
Native push: APNs/FCM token registration listeners (registration/received/action) report token to backend (nativePush.ts); wired on login + push-toggle, torn down on logout

Native BLE: bleManager now has dual backend - Web Bluetooth (PWA) + @capacitor-community/bluetooth-le (native), selected by isNativePlatform; same GATT UUIDs/callbacks (native transfer layer needs real-device validation)

Native (Capacitor) permissions: permissions.ts + notifications now use native plugins on iOS/Android (push/local-notifications, camera, bluetooth-le) via isNativePlatform branch; Android manifest permissions added; iOS Info.plist + setup documented in docs/NATIVE_SETUP.md

iOS/Android adaptation: ship real PNG icons (192/512/apple-touch-180/badge-96) — iOS ignores SVG home-screen icons and Android notification icons must be raster; manifest now PNG + maskable; apple-touch-icon PNG + apple-mobile-web-app-title; notifications use existing PNG icon/badge; outage notif goes through SW (iOS PWA compatible)

Fix Real-Time Power chart: history never loaded (useHistoryFetcher checked res.success which does not exist -> now isApiSuccess(res.code)); widen zoom/pan gesture area to whole chart + add desktop wheel-zoom

Web Push (client side): subscribe via VAPID + report PushSubscription to backend on enabling notifications; idempotent re-sync on launch; unsubscribe on logout. Workbox SW now importScripts sw-push.js so server pushes display when app is closed/locked (requires backend VAPID push to deliver)

Low battery: banner threshold text now reflects Settings value; global low-battery monitor polls all devices app-wide and pushes a system notification when SOC crosses the threshold (fires while app runs in foreground or background; 30-min per-device throttle)
Insights: remove Battery Health card (ring + Battery/Temperature/Days/Health tiles)
Unify battery remaining/to-full time across app: DebugParamsPage + DevicePage low-battery banner now use batteryTimeLabel (capacity=acInvOutputPowerx2 Wh); removed duplicate/old buggy formulas
Battery ring time estimate: default rated capacity fallback 5000Wh -> 1000Wh when nameplate not cached
Fix battery ring time estimate: use real capacity (acInvOutputPower x2 Wh) so remaining/to-full = energyWh / power (was treating SOC%% as Wh, e.g. wrong 11m)

UI polish: tabular numbers on live values (no jitter), scale-on-press feedback on buttons, balanced heading/body text wrap, skip page-enter animation on first load, replace transition-all with explicit properties
Remove swipe-to-back gesture on Overview and Device Monitor pages (prevent accidental navigation)

Real-Time Power chart: real time X-axis (12am/4am/8am/12pm/4pm/8pm/12am), pinch-to-zoom + pan (min 1 hour), today's API history (paginated + IndexedDB cache); realtime value shown only in top-right badge
Standardize backend API params (isApiSuccess, normalizeCountryCode, CaptchaIntent) + document page/label/API conventions in CLAUDE.md
Unify UI metric labels: battery=Battery(%), AC input=AC, temperature=Temperature(°F); remove dead pages (DeviceInfoPage, PeakShavingPage)
Device list: fetch every device's battery on enter + refresh all every 60s (was only updating one device due to single-slot state)
Device monitor: back arrow + swipe now return to Device list (was going back in history)
Unified page transition animations across all routes (no flicker switching Overview ↔ Device list)
Overview: horizontal swipe gesture returns to Device list page
Fix: username update 'illegal argument' (no userId sent to iotUserInfo)
Fix: password update 'illegal argument' (no userId sent to authPassword)
EmailJS configured for in-app feedback form
